heading: Banning of isopropal nitrite and other nitrites
label: 2057b Banning of isopropal nitrite and other nitrites
notes: References in TextThe Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act, referred to in subsec. (b), is , , which is classified generally to chapter 9 (§ 301 et seq.) of Title 21, Food and Drugs. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see and Tables.act June 25, 1938, ch. 67552 Stat. 1040section 301 of Title 21
CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Crime Control Act of 1990, and not as part of the Consumer Product Safety Act which comprises this chapter.
amendments: References in TextThe Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act, referred to in subsec. (b), is , , which is classified generally to chapter 9 (§ 301 et seq.) of Title 21, Food and Drugs. children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), volatile alkyl nitrite shall be considered a banned hazardous product under .section 2057 of this title

children.heading: Lawful purposes
children.label: b Lawful purposes
children.text: For the purposes of , it shall not be unlawful for any person to manufacture for sale, offer for sale, distribute in commerce, or import into the United States volatile alkyl nitrites for any commercial purpose or any other purpose approved under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [ et seq.].section 2057 of this title21 U.S.C. 301

children.heading: “Commercial purpose” defined
children.label: c “Commercial purpose” defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “commercial purpose” means any commercial purpose other than for the production of consumer products containing volatile alkyl nitrites that may be used for inhaling or otherwise introducing volatile alkyl nitrites into the human body for euphoric or physical effects.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: d Effective date
children.text: This section shall take effect 90 days after .November 29, 1990
